name: production-viewer-osm-buildings-nyc
description: A realistic production setup: viewer with terrain, OSM buildings, camera positioned over a specific city. Tests the skill's ability to guide the LLM through combining multiple factory helpers and camera positioning in a single coherent setup.
prompt: Build a production CesiumJS viewer showing New York City with 3D OSM buildings. The camera should be positioned to show lower Manhattan from a slight angle, looking roughly north. Disable the animation and timeline widgets since this is a static visualization. Use world terrain.
expected_behaviors: Calls createOsmBuildingsAsync() and adds result to scene.primitives
Positions camera near lower Manhattan (approx lon: -74.01, lat: 40.70-40.71)
Uses camera.flyTo or camera.setView with appropriate altitude (500-2000m for city view)
Sets heading/pitch for angled view (not straight down)
visual_expectations: A 3D view of lower Manhattan with visible building extrusions from OSM data. The camera should be at a moderate altitude showing the urban landscape with terrain. Buildings should appear as 3D blocks/shapes. The Statue of Liberty area or Brooklyn Bridge area may be visible depending on exact camera position. No animation controls or timeline visible.

programmatic_checks.description: OSM buildings factory helper is used
Buildings tileset is added to scene
Longitude is approximately correct for NYC (negative = west)
Latitude is approximately correct for NYC (~40.7)

screenshots.description: Initial load — globe and terrain should be visible
After flyTo completes — NYC buildings and terrain should be fully rendered

notes: This is the 'hero' scenario — the most common real-world setup. Common failures: (1) forgetting 'await' on createOsmBuildingsAsync, (2) wrong coordinate sign for NYC longitude, (3) camera altitude too high (100km) or too low (10m), (4) not importing createOsmBuildingsAsync.
